title: Accumulator-Aware Post-Training Quantization for Large Language Models
preview: Addresses numerical overflow in low-precision accumulators during LLM GEMM operations, enabling safe INT4 quantization without accuracy loss.
summary: Identifies that standard PTQ methods ignore accumulator bit-width constraints in hardware Tensor Cores, leading to silent overflow errors at INT4 and below. Proposes accumulator-aware weight clipping and scaling strategies that maintain numerical correctness while achieving high compression ratios.
datacenter: Directly relevant to INT4/FP4 inference deployment on H100 and MI300X hardware. Accumulator overflow is a real production risk for quantized model serving at scale — this provides a principled mitigation.
metrics: Key result signal: elimination of accumulator overflow events with less than 0.5% perplexity degradation versus FP16 baseline.
